questions.q: In an /etc/group line 'GroupP:x:516:patrick', what are the four colon-separated fields?
options: Group name : password marker (x) : GID : supplementary members
Group name : GID : password : primary user
questions.why: The fields are: group name, password placeholder (x - real password is in /etc/gshadow), GID, and the supplementary members.

questions.q: In /etc/group, where does the 'x' in the second field indicate the group password is really stored?
questions.why: The x is a placeholder; the actual (encrypted) group password lives in /etc/gshadow.

questions.q: The last field of an /etc/group line lists which users?
options: The supplementary members of the group (not the users for whom it is the primary group)
The users for whom this is the primary group
questions.why: Only supplementary members are listed here; primary-group membership is recorded in /etc/passwd.

questions.q: In an /etc/gshadow line 'GroupA:$6$...:alain:rockstar', what is the third field (alain)?
questions.why: The gshadow fields are: group name, encrypted password, group administrator, and supplementary members.

questions.q: What does an '!' in the password field of /etc/gshadow mean?
options: The password is locked, so no one can use a password to access the group
The group is a system group
questions.why: A ! locks the group password; members don't need it anyway, so this is common.

questions.q: What must be true about /etc/group and /etc/gshadow?
options: Each line in /etc/group must have a corresponding line in /etc/gshadow (names match one-to-one)
They are independent and need not match
gshadow may list groups that group does not
Only root groups need appear in both
questions.why: The group names in /etc/group and /etc/gshadow must correspond one-to-one, line for line.
